text:
Explore (Digging In)

Providing for Individual Needs

As you move around the room listening to students discuss the diagrams, listen for ho they interpret the pictures into quantities.

Note: Give students time to experiment with the grids and tape diagrams before offering suggestions.

Have Learning Aid Template: Blank 100s Grids and Learning Aid Template: Blank Grids and Tape Diagrams available for students to use as needed.

Students may write comparison statements for the diagrams and then interpret the comparison statement as a percent.

Suggest Questions

  • What would be a comparison statement to describe the diagram?
  • What would be a part-to-whole ratio for that diagram?
  • If a percent is an amount out of 100, how can I write that as a percent?

• Various answers. AN example for What If...? Situation A Diagram A might be "For every 70 white squares, there are 30 green."
  • There are 70 white squares for every 100 squares.
  • 70% of the squares are white.
